reports.title: The Ceasefire That Isn't
reports.subtitle: Both sides are calling it peace while actively fighting. The real question is who blinks first before the next deadline.

reports.title: The $166 Billion Bait and Switch
reports.subtitle: The Supreme Court struck down the tariffs. The refunds are real. The question nobody will answer is who actually gets the money.

reports.title: The Fed Chair Who Won't Say What He Thinks
reports.subtitle: Kevin Warsh's confirmation hearing confirmed one thing: he has no intention of telling Congress, or the markets, what he will actually do.

reports.title: America Is Winning the AI Race and Losing the War
reports.subtitle: China isn't trying to build the best AI model. It is building the best factory. Those are different competitions, and the US has barely started the second one.

reports.title: Trump to Britain: Protect Apple or Pay Tariffs
reports.subtitle: The UK's digital services tax is not a trade dispute. It is a test of whether any US ally can tax American tech companies without US retaliation.

reports.title: The Kids App Ban That Big Tech Will Actually Win
reports.subtitle: Norway and Turkey both moved today to block children from social media. The courts found Meta liable last month. The laws will pass. The platforms will comply on paper and change almost nothing.

reports.title: Samsung's Workers Want Their Cut of the AI Boom
reports.subtitle: Thirty thousand chip workers are threatening an 18-day strike over profit-sharing. Samsung's Q1 operating profit rose 755%. The math is not complicated. The power dynamic is.

reports.title: Hungary's Veto Ended the Moment Its Oil Came Back On
reports.subtitle: The EU unlocked a 90 billion euro loan for Ukraine this week after months of deadlock. Hungary and Slovakia dropped their objections the same day the Druzhba pipeline was repaired. That is not a coincidence. That is a transaction.

reports.title: Trump Extended a Ceasefire That Lebanon Cannot Keep
reports.subtitle: The Oval Office meeting produced a three-week truce extension and a photo opportunity. It did not produce a party that controls Hezbollah. That remains the only thing that matters.

reports.title: No One in Washington Wants to Own the Haiti Decision
reports.subtitle: The Supreme Court hears arguments April 29 on whether the Trump administration can deport Haitians back to a country in active gang war. Congress just voted to stop it. Both institutions are trying to hand the problem to the other.
